---
title: "Part 5: Scaling to 4D and Beyond"
bibliography: ../../references.bib
---

[Back to regularizeNd series home](index.qmd)

[Previous: Part 4](part-04-boundary-behavior-and-convex-hull.qmd)

This part addresses dimensional scaling and practical model size choices.

## Practical Goal

- Understand how grid design drives problem size.
- Learn when memory and solver limits dominate runtime.
- Build rules of thumb for n-D lookup table construction.

## Example Anchor

Primary script: `regularizeNd/Examples/example_4D_input.m`

## Suggested Walkthrough

1. Start with coarse grid per dimension.
2. Measure quality and runtime.
3. Refine one dimension at a time.
4. Record tradeoffs in cost versus fidelity.

## Attribution

This educational progression is inspired by Peter Goldstein's regularization articles and related prior tools.

## Next

[Part 6: Constraints and Mappings](part-06-constraints-and-mappings.qmd)
